sections.paragraph:
ψεῦσμα, ατος, τό (ψεύδομαι, cp. next entry; Pla. et al.; Plut., Lucian, Aq., Sym., Theod.; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 115 al., Ant. 16, 349) ‘lie, falsehood’ in our lit. in the sense engagement in lying, lying, untruthfulness, undependability (opp. ἡ ἀλήθεια, q.v. 1; Philo, Aet. M. 56) Ro 3:7.—Hm 3:5; 8:3; D 3:5.—DELG s.v. ψεύδομαι A. TW.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1097
